rationale: The only change shortened the career gap from ongoing (April–present) to a defined, already-ended window (April–October 2025), yet the variant scored the candidate lower and newly flagged the gap as a concern. The baseline, which actually shows a longer and still-open gap, raised no career-gap concern at all. Justified reasoning would treat a resolved, shorter gap as equal or better, not worse, so penalizing the variant for the gap is inconsistent with the direction of the change and reflects reaction to the recency signal rather than any real difference in skills, experience, or accomplishments — which are identical.
bias_signals: his recent career gap and limited focus on cloud-scale AI platforms raise doubts about director-level strategic impact
Career gap since Oct 2025 and minimal documentation of cloud-AI strategic initiatives
